claim_summary: TBI Africa reported a service-delivery pledge and staff commitment message by KEDCO MD.
Interview stated KEDCO maintained public service delivery during COVID-19 and customers saw improved supply.
Daily Trust interview described KEDCO service continuity and safety priorities during COVID-19.
ICT sector growth during the recession period was cited in official achievements materials.
A public legislative guide includes Senate committee references involving FERMA and Alkali; direct FERMA agency executive leadership was not verified in the seed public evidence.
NCC-linked statement reported ICT contribution to GDP at 14.42 percent in Q2 2021.
NCC-linked statement reported broadband penetration at 40.01 percent by September 2021.
Public report stated 80 million Nigerians had broadband access by December 2021.
USPF ministry-achievements page reported 1667 projects/centres established and 455 ongoing.
NITDA article credited agency development and GDP relevance in the digital economy ecosystem.
No direct electricity/water utility management role identified in the included public sources.
USPF ministry-achievements page reported more than 61 million unique NIN enrolments.
USPF ministry-achievements page reported more than 219000 direct digital skills beneficiaries.
KEDCO role supports regional commerce through distribution service, but seed data lacks audited economic impact figures.
Blueprint reported inspection of new transformers/accessories and feeder construction to improve service delivery.
Reported transformer/accessory procurement and feeder construction indicate operational improvement activity.
NERC Q2 2022 report listed Kano DisCo metering performance at 22.52 percent.
NERC Q4 2022 reported metering, billing, and interruption were major complaint categories across DisCos.
Daily Trust reported Gwamna statement that staff training and retraining were part of KEDCO transformation strategy.
Punch reported Alkali criticized the Port Harcourt-Maiduguri contractor after stating work around Port Harcourt-Aba was below expected pace and about 45-47 percent in the inspected section.
NITT reported Alkali's position that transport can sustain the economy, reduce unemployment, lower mobility costs, and reduce movement delays for goods and services.
The ministry reported plans to restore freight on the Lagos-Kano narrow gauge to support cargo movement to Kano's Dala Inland Dry Port.
The transportation ministry reported Alkali's inspection and assurance on the first 387km Kano-Daura segment of the Kano-Maradi rail project.
The ministry stated Alkali inspected Kano-Maradi and Kaduna-Kano rail works with engineers, consultants, and contractors for on-ground assessment.

indicator: Kaduna-Kano rail financing and port rail links
claim_summary: Independent reported Alkali's statement that financing had been secured for Kaduna-Kano standard gauge and that Badagry, TinCan, Apapa, Lekki, Ijebu Ode and Kajola rail links were initiated.
The performance bond included the Nigerian Railway Corporation and Nigerian Institute of Transport Technology among agencies aligning roles, KPIs, manpower, research, and rail development programmes.

indicator: Official public profile and office history
claim_summary: The Federal Ministry of Transportation profile records Alkali's background, Gombe State executive roles, senatorial service, and appointment as Minister of Transportation on 16 August 2023.
The ministry reported that Alkali signed a performance bond with directors and agency heads to cascade KPIs and annual appraisal for the transportation sector.

indicator: Transport manpower and research mandate through NITT
claim_summary: The ministry reported NITT commitments under the performance bond to provide required manpower, innovation, and transport-sector research.

indicator: Transport ministry capital budget release constraint
claim_summary: TheCable reported Alkali saying only about 1 percent of the ministry's 2025 capital budget was released, while 70 percent of projects rolled into 2026 due to funding constraints.
TheCable reported a legislative committee statement that the ministry secured private-sector funding commitments of about $200m despite limited capital releases.
Federal information reporting stated that the first National Land Transport Policy had been validated by stakeholders and approved by the Federal Executive Council.

indicator: Smart mobility and logistics technology agenda
claim_summary: Government information reporting linked Alkali's agenda to smart mobility, intelligent transport systems, blockchain-enabled logistics, NITT logistics-drone testing, and rail unbundling.

indicator: Public response to railway diversion allegation
claim_summary: Punch reported the ministry's denial of allegations that a $3bn railway project was diverted to Gombe, and its explanation of project sections and funding constraints.
